chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يَطَأُ عَلَى الْعَذِرَةِ أَوْ غَيْرِهَا مِنَ الْقَذَرِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 24 – The man treads upon the faeces or other than it, from the filth

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور وآخره مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/123)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور وآخره مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/123)
text: الرَّجُلِ يَطَأُ فِي الْعَذِرَةِ أَوِ الْبَوْلِ أَ يُعِيدُ الْوُضُوءَ قَالَ لَا وَ لَكِنْ يَغْسِلُ مَا أَصَابَهُ .
وَ فِي رِوَايَةٍ أُخْرَى إِذَا كَانَ جَافّاً فَلَا يَغْسِلُهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Muhammad, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Muhammad Bin Sinan, from Ibn Muskan, from Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws regarding the man who treads upon the excrement, or the urine, should he be repeating the Ablution (Wudhu)?’ Heasws said: ‘No, but he should wash whatever it hit’.
And in another report, ‘(He<sup>asws</sup> said): ‘When it was dehydrated, so he may not wash it’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Muhammad has narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Muhammad ibn Sinan from ibn Muskan from al-Halabiy who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of one who steps on feces or urine whether he must take Wudu’ again?’ He (the Imam) replied, ‘No, but one must wash the polluted area clean.’”
